verdict: 2026-06-27 07:14:40 PDT — The light seam is the right shape for this repo. A dispatcher now would add a new deployed lib/script pattern, install drift surface, and adapter contract before the agy backend constraints are known, which conflicts with the repo's deliberate one-hook/no-script-suite minimalism. Deferring the dispatcher does not create a worse seam as long as the codex branch stays exact and agy fails loudly. The design sketch is sound, but two AC/test notes should be tightened so the story does not accidentally force the heavy seam or an impossible golden test.

findings.title: Callable resolver requirement smuggles in the heavy seam
findings.claim: The light-seam design says selector resolution is a documented rule the skills follow, but the test notes require a callable/inspectable unit. In this repo, reviewer behavior lives in Markdown skills; a test-only resolver would not prove the skills use it, while a deployed helper would establish the new script/lib precedent the design is intentionally avoiding.
findings.alternative: Keep the runtime seam in the skills for this story: document the resolver rule once in the relevant skill instructions, require each call site to branch codex vs agy, and have the gate assert observable artifacts/text fixtures: default codex, reviewer-token parsing examples, agy STOP text, and unchanged codex command envelope. Save a real shared dispatcher/resolver for the agy-backend story when it has executable backend logic to centralize.
findings.win: Avoids adding a dead test helper or new deployed script pattern while still preserving the concrete safety invariant: no silent agy fallback and no changed codex invocation.

findings.title: Byte-for-byte codex preservation conflicts with neutral prompt prose
findings.claim: AC4/test notes ask for behavior-identical, character-equivalent codex commands, while AC6 intentionally changes prompt preambles from Codex-specific role language to neutral independent-reviewer language. If the golden compare includes the prompt string, the ACs fight each other.
findings.alternative: Define AC4 as preserving the codex command envelope and artifact semantics: `codex exec -s read-only`, absolute schema path, repo-relative `-o`, `codexModel`, artifact names, and `</dev/null`. Treat the prompt wording change as the explicit AC6 behavior change, or exclude the prompt body from the golden comparison.
findings.win: Eliminates an implementation/test ambiguity without weakening the important regression guard around the fragile codex flags and path split.
